title: Structural Feasibility Confidence (SFC): From Confidence Thickness to Structural Feasibility Metrics
description:
Structural Feasibility Confidence (SFC) investigates one of the most fundamental questions in engineering and future Artificial Intelligence:

How can an intelligent system determine whether a target structure is actually feasible before the complete solution has been constructed?

Unlike conventional AI systems that primarily estimate similarity, SFC introduces the concept of Structural Feasibility Confidence, where confidence is interpreted as accumulated structural evidence supporting feasible structural evolution rather than isolated probabilistic estimation.

The repository develops the theory through three progressive stages: (1) observable engineering phenomena such as Confidence Thickness, Puzzle Reasoning, and Gap Bridging; (2) the underlying Structural Feasibility Metric based on hierarchical feasibility aggregation using differential trees, concept coverage validation, evolution logic validation, and immutable constraint validation; and (3) engineering implications including Autonomous Contracting, Structural Feasible Regions, and future Structural Feasibility Metrics.

SFC naturally connects Structural Intelligence, Function Tunnel Intelligence, Universal Typing and Naming, Calling Graphs, Structural Cognitive Runtime, and Gap Bridging into a unified feasibility-oriented reasoning framework.

The repository further argues that many engineering, scientific, and collaborative reasoning problems are fundamentally feasibility problems rather than similarity problems, providing one possible computational foundation for future Autonomous Structural Intelligence (ASI), Brain Unit collaboration, executable contracts, and eventually Computable Cooperation.
